response:
Wade spent eight dollars on his two drinks, leaving eighteen dollars for the three sandwiches he purchased today. Each sandwich cost six dollars because eighteen divided by three equals six in this simple math lab problem. The total cost matches the twenty-six dollars he paid at the register for his food and drinks.

***

This calculation shows that the price per sandwich is six dollars, which fits the budget perfectly for his trip. You can verify the math by adding the drink costs and sandwich costs to reach the total sum. [location] [date] [time] [name] are the variables you might need to track for future stops.
